title: Brandy snaps
subtitle: Lacy rolled biscuits, crisp and golden
excerpt: Brandy snaps are thin caramel biscuits baked flat, then rolled around the handle of a wooden spoon while still warm. Once cool they become brittle and hollow, ready to fill with whipped cream.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, includes a brandy snap recipe in the confectionery chapter; the biscuit has been a British fairground and festive staple since at least the mid-nineteenth century. Florence White, Good Things in England (1932), notes regional variations with ginger and lemon, which are preserved here.

recipe.batchNotes: Bake in batches of 6 to 8 to allow time to roll. The rolled snaps keep unfilled in an airtight tin for up to a week.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Roll and store unfilled. Fill up to 30 minutes before serving; they go soggy with cream inside if left overnight.

content.text: Brandy snaps are a race against time. They come out of the oven soft and pliable for about 90 seconds, then they harden fast. You roll them around the handle of a wooden spoon one at a time, working quickly. If one sets before you can roll it, return it to the warm oven for a minute and it will soften again.

Melt the butter, golden syrup, and demerara sugar together in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves. Remove from the heat and cool for 5 minutes. Stir in the flour, ginger, and lemon juice until smooth.

Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Line a baking tray with baking paper. Drop 6 to 8 teaspoons of batter onto the tray, spaced 10 cm apart: the biscuits spread considerably. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes, until the biscuits are deep amber and the edges are lacy and set. Watch closely after 7 minutes; they burn in seconds.

Let the biscuits cool on the tray for 30 to 60 seconds, until they are just firm enough to lift but still pliable. Working one at a time, lift with a palette knife and roll around the handle of a wooden spoon. Hold for 10 seconds, then slide off and place on a rack to set completely. If any biscuit hardens before you can roll it, return the tray to the oven for 60 seconds to soften. Repeat with the remaining batter in batches. Once all the snaps are cool and set, fill with whipped cream using a piping bag.
